headlines.title: Mounting frustrations over Wisconsin police shooting
headlines.description: Authorities say Corey Ruiz was shot and killed by a police officer in Madison, Wisconsin. Multiple videos capture the incident from different angles. After a chase and a struggle, police say Ruiz pulled out a knife. NBC News’ Shaquille Brewster reports from the scene.

headlines.title: President Trump announces new tariffs on dozens of countries
headlines.description: President Trump announced new 10% to 12.5% tariffs on 60 countries over accusations of "forced labor." Those new duties will take effect at 12:01 a.m. ET on Friday morning.

headlines.title: FDA panel recommends controversial treatments
headlines.description: A controversial new FDA panel recommendation could affect the sale of peptides—popular treatments marketed for benefits ranging from muscle growth to weight loss. NBC News’ Anne Thompson explains why not everyone thinks they’re a good idea.

headlines.title: House votes to end Iran war, but Senate Republicans vote to let Trump continue waging it
headlines.description: The House approved a nonbinding proposal to end the Iran war, directing President Donald Trump to remove armed forces from hostilities.

headlines.title: White House says Saudi Arabia nuclear deal contingent on joining Abraham Accords
headlines.description: During a press briefing, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt echoed a social media post from President Trump that said any nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia would be contingent on them joining the Abraham Accords. NBC News' Monica Alba reports from the White House on the potential deal and the White House's approach.

headlines.title: Stabbings near New York City’s Central Park
headlines.description: Terrifying moments in New York City this afternoon when two people were stabbed near Central Park, just feet from a synagogue. Witnesses say the attacker yelled Allahu-Akbar. NBC News’ Erin McLaughlin has the latest on the investigation

headlines.title: Justice Department agrees to withdraw subpoenas of New York Times journalists over Air Force One reporting
headlines.description: A judge repeatedly questioned why prosecutors had issued the subpoenas before pursuing alternative investigative steps.

headlines.title: Deadly flooding in the East
headlines.description: At least two people have lost their lives after widespread flooding in West Virginia. We're also tracking Tropical Storm Bertha as it hits Texas. NBC News’ Priscilla Thompson is in the storm zone.

headlines.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
headlines.description: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: FDA panel recommends easing restrictions on several peptides
headlines.description: The panel went against the conclusions of the agency’s career scientists, who said there wasn’t enough evidence to support the move.

headlines.title: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul explains why she signed the first statewide moratorium on data centers
headlines.description: Speaking with NBC News’ “Here’s the Scoop,” Hochul said that she’s responding to the concerns of localities and that New York doesn’t need data centers to support the AI boom.
